metadata.title: Mithril Partial Sync UX Refinement Tasks (Phase 2)
metadata.description: High-level, validation-gated implementation plan for the Mithril partial sync UX refinement sprint (issue #10 Phase 2). The backend, IPC, store, and overlay already exist on feat/mithril-partial-sync-ux-refinement; this sprint refines the UX to (a) prompt the user when significantly behind the tip and (b) close the 35 research gaps plus the 13 issue-#10-comment items. Tasks are organized so task-ux-1xx maps to phase-1, task-ux-2xx to phase-2, and so on, matching the task-plans-ux-refinement/phase-N subdirectory layout.
metadata.importantNote: This is a refinement sprint, not a rebuild. Every locked safety boundary from the backend PRD and research-19 §5 MUST be preserved: staged-only restore; the Boundary A vs B/C1/C2 recovery model rendered strictly from backend allowedRecoveryActions (never inferred from status names); confirmation precedes start; no auto-trigger; no renderer-computed threshold (the backend owns behind-ness); latest-snapshot-only with no snapshot-selection UI and no user-facing storage-location picker (engineering scratch/staging placement is in scope per D7/BUG3); supported networks mainnet/preprod/preview; serialized mutation lock; marker-driven startup recovery; and no synthetic throughput/remaining-time/overall-% over IPC. Locked decision #16 is intentionally amended by PRD D9 (post-dismiss backend reset-to-idle + staging cleanup + node-start-verified marker). Per PRD D7, the backend-correctness de-risking track and the full manual QA matrix are REFERENCED deployment gates that this PRD does not fully re-specify; tasks task-ux-701 and task-ux-702 represent those gates and require operator/user involvement rather than autonomous implementation.

phases.name: Cross-Process Availability Contract And Behind-ness Signal
phases.description: Add the backend-owned availability read model (kill-switch state + certified-immutable-gap behind-ness) and its dedicated IPC channel so the renderer can gate all partial-sync UI truthfully without ever computing a threshold. Implements PRD D2 and D3. This phase is foundational: every renderer discovery surface in phase 3 gates on it.

tasks.title: Add MithrilPartialSyncAvailability read model, dedicated channel, and kill-switch exposure
tasks.description: Introduce a backend-owned availability read model carrying both the kill-switch state and the D2 behind-ness signal, delivered on a dedicated channel kept separate from the in-flight MithrilPartialSyncStatusSnapshot (which describes an operation, not static availability). Surface launcherConfig.mithrilPartialSyncEnabled into it and enable mithrilPartialSyncEnabled = true on this branch's launcher config for manual testing. Closes blocker #1's contract half (kill switch invisible to the renderer). Implements PRD D3 and the contract surface for D2.
implementationNotes: Define `type MithrilPartialSyncAvailability = { isEnabled: boolean; isSignificantlyBehind: boolean; behindByImmutables?: number }` in `mithril-partial-sync.types.ts`; the renderer receives only this and never thresholds `behindByImmutables`.
Add a dedicated availability channel (one-shot request at store setup plus a periodic refresh while syncing); do NOT widen the existing status snapshot channel.
Follow the existing Daedalus IPC patterns already used by the partial-sync status/action channels; add both main and renderer wrappers.
`isEnabled` comes from `launcherConfig.mithrilPartialSyncEnabled`, already enforced at the coordinator level (`chainStorageCoordinator.ts:37-38,375-379`).

Set `mithrilPartialSyncEnabled = true` at `nix/internal/launcher-config.nix:409` for this branch's full manual testing (PRD D3 rollout context); production default and network-scoping are deferred to the readiness gate, so document the change as branch-scoped.
This task wires the contract and the kill-switch field; the behind-ness computation that populates `isSignificantlyBehind`/`behindByImmutables` is task-ux-102.
Update `.agent/system/api-endpoints.md` per the IPC documentation workflow.
testCases: Renderer one-shot availability query returns isEnabled reflecting launcher config
Availability channel is separate from the status snapshot channel
isEnabled false when kill switch off; true on this branch
acceptance: Shared MithrilPartialSyncAvailability type compiles and is exported.
A dedicated availability channel exists with main and renderer wrappers, separate from MithrilPartialSyncStatusSnapshot.
mithrilPartialSyncEnabled is surfaced into the availability read model and set true on this branch's launcher config.
api-endpoints.md documents the new channel.

tasks.title: Compute the certified-immutable-gap behind-ness signal (cached, no restore)
tasks.description: Teach the backend to compute isSignificantlyBehind from the certified-immutable gap and feed it (plus the raw behindByImmutables) into the availability read model from task-ux-101. The signal fires only when a real certified range exists, so the user is never nudged toward a partial sync that would dead-end in PARTIAL_SYNC_NO_CERTIFIED_RANGE. Implements PRD D2. Closes the contract+backend half of blocker #2.
implementationNotes: gap = latestCertifiedImmutableNumber - localImm, where localImm is the highest parseable immutable file number on disk. Reuse the EXISTING seams: `resolveLatestSnapshotMetadata` (`MithrilPartialSyncService.ts:571`) for the latest certified immutable number and `derivePartialSyncRange` (`mithrilPartialSyncPreflight.ts:143`) for the gap math; do not add new restore machinery.

isSignificantlyBehind = gap >= THRESHOLD_IMMUTABLES; THRESHOLD_IMMUTABLES is backend-owned and tunable via launcher config (default ~= one epoch-equivalent of immutable files, conservative; calibrate down during QA).
Reuse one cached Mithril aggregator query refreshed periodically while syncing; do NOT tight-poll the aggregator (Non-Functional requirement).
Renderer never sees or computes the threshold; expose only isSignificantlyBehind and optionally behindByImmutables for honest copy.
When no certified range exists, isSignificantlyBehind must be false (truthfulness rationale, D2).
testCases: gap >= threshold yields isSignificantlyBehind true with behindByImmutables set
gap < threshold yields false
No certified range available yields false (never nudges into a dead-end)
Aggregator query is cached, not tight-polled
acceptance: Backend computes isSignificantlyBehind from the certified-immutable gap reusing the existing latest-snapshot resolver.
THRESHOLD_IMMUTABLES is launcher-config-tunable and backend-owned.
The availability read model carries isSignificantlyBehind and behindByImmutables.
The behind-ness Mithril query is cached and low-frequency.

phases.name: Backend UX-Enabling Correctness
phases.description: Land the UX-enabling backend changes the renderer depends on: relocate staging onto the chain volume with a disk preflight, finalize the success path (reset-to-idle + staging cleanup + truthful marker), guarantee cancel always re-emits status, and make the native startup dialog the single interrupted-cutover recovery surface. Implements PRD D5a, D5f (backend half), D7/BUG3, and D9.

tasks.title: Colocate partial-sync staging on the resolved chain volume + disk-space preflight
tasks.description: Derive partial-sync staging from the resolved chain work directory (a sibling of the managed chain) instead of the hardcoded top-level state directory, so it honors backend PRD line 250, fills the correct volume on relocated/symlinked chains, and makes cutover an intra-volume rename rather than a cross-device copy. Add a disk-space preflight. Implements PRD D7/BUG3. No user-facing storage picker (the non-goal is about user snapshot pickers, not scratch placement).
implementationNotes: Change the staging root in `_getStagingRootPath()` (`MithrilPartialSyncService.ts:567-568`, which joins `stateDirectoryPath` + the `PARTIAL_SYNC_STAGING_DIRECTORY_NAME` constant at `:57` -- not a string literal) to derive from the resolved chain work dir / `_activeWorkDir` (`:153`), so staging is a sibling of the managed chain (gap #42).
Keep the existing guard that rejects any staging path inside the managed chain subtree.
Add a disk-space preflight before download begins; surface a precise, retriable preflight error if space is insufficient (renderer copy handled by the error-copy map task).
Preserve the staged-only restore lock; staging must never mutate the live chain in place.
testCases: Staging path resolves under the chain work dir, not the top-level state dir
Relocated/symlinked chain stages on the same volume as the chain
Disk-space preflight fails closed with an actionable error when space is insufficient
Cutover is an intra-volume operation when staging is colocated
acceptance: Staging is colocated on the resolved chain volume, honoring backend PRD line 250.
A disk-space preflight runs before any destructive/download step.
No user-facing storage picker is introduced.

tasks.title: Success-path finalization: finalize channel, reset-to-idle, staging cleanup, and node-start-verified marker (Boundary C2)
tasks.description: Make a verified partial-sync success finalize the operation rather than parking in completed. Add a dedicated finalize/dismiss IPC request channel (a fifth partial-sync action channel) routed through MithrilController to MithrilPartialSyncService; on success-overlay dismiss the service idempotently runs _resetToIdleStatus + fs.remove(stagingRoot) + clearMithrilPartialSyncMarker. Write the node-start-verified marker after the node is proven RUNNING (Option A), activating the dead Boundary C2 startup branch, and defer the marker clear to the dismiss-driven finalize. Extend the C2 startup branch to also reclaim leftover staging on a close-without-dismiss. Implements PRD D9 (amends locked decision #16); fixes BUG1, BUG2, and gap #44.
implementationNotes: Add a finalize/dismiss request channel alongside start/cancel/restart-normal/wipe; route it MithrilController -> MithrilPartialSyncService. This is genuinely new behavior with no seam to reuse, so a new channel is warranted (does not contradict the retry=start reuse decision).

Implement the D9 'Ordered finalization sequence' (PRD D9) -- the canonical task-ux-202 doc owns the exact step order. Seams: `finalizeInstalledNodeStart` (`mithrilPartialSyncNodeStartup.ts:147-172`), `writeMithrilPartialSyncMarker`/`clearMithrilPartialSyncMarker` (`mithrilPartialSyncMarker.ts`), `_resetToIdleStatus` (`MithrilPartialSyncService.ts`, already used by cancel/restart/wipe). Net effect: verified success stamps node-start-verified + emits completed; the dismiss-driven finalize does reset-to-idle + fs.remove(stagingRoot) + clear-marker idempotently (gap #41 staging-leftover).

Do NOT reset to idle at verified success: pushing idle would drop shouldShowOverlay and hide the success screen before the user sees it (violates lock #16). The dismiss-driven finalize is the authoritative reset/cleanup trigger.

Activate the never-written node-start-verified state (`mithrilPartialSyncMarker.ts:6-9`); `finalizeInstalledNodeStart` currently jumps from C1 straight to clearing the marker (`:165`), leaving the C2 branch (`mithrilPartialSyncNodeStartup.ts:60-63`) dead.
Extend the C2 startup branch (`:60-63`) to also fs.remove(stagingRoot) so a close-without-dismiss / crash reclaims leftover staging on next launch.
The verified-success marker stamp + completed emission run in the main-process startup path; the dismiss-driven finalize runs in MithrilPartialSyncService.
The renderer half (dismissCompletedOverlay invokes this channel; isActionBlocked derived from isWorking) is task-ux-404 and task-ux-301 respectively.
testCases: Verified success stamps node-start-verified then emits completed; marker not cleared until dismiss
Dismiss-driven finalize resets to idle, removes stagingRoot, and clears the marker exactly once
Finalize channel is idempotent (safe when already idle)
Close-without-dismiss leaves node-start-verified; next launch C2 branch resumes normal boot and reclaims staging
Interrupted-after-verification run resumes a normal boot (C2), not a C1 re-drive
acceptance: A finalize/dismiss channel exists and is routed to the service idempotently.
node-start-verified is written after the node is proven RUNNING; Boundary C2 startup branch is live.
Verified success keeps the completed overlay visible; reset + staging removal + marker clear happen on dismiss.
Close-without-dismiss reclaims leftover staging via the C2 branch on next launch.
Locked decision #16 amendment (D9) is implemented as documented.

tasks.title: Guarantee cancel always re-emits status (backend half of cancel-during-stop fix)
tasks.description: Ensure a cancel request always results in a status emission so the renderer never sticks on an optimistic frame. Today service.cancel early-returns while _activeWorkDir/_currentProcess are still null during the node-stop window (MithrilPartialSyncService.ts:241-247), so the UI looks dead. Implements the backend half of PRD D5f (gap #39). True abort-during-stop (a cancel flag honored before the node-stop handler) remains deferred to the backend-correctness track (D7).
implementationNotes: On any cancel request, re-emit/sync the current status even when the early-return path is taken, so the renderer can resync rather than stick.
Do not change the post-cutover cancel hard-rejection (`MithrilPartialSyncService.ts:249-253`); that lock stays (gap #9 is handled in the renderer).
Optionally have the backend emit stopping-node when it actually stops the node so the status is truthful rather than purely renderer-optimistic (polish; pairs with D8#19).
The renderer disable-Cancel-during-stop tooltip + always-syncStatus-after-cancel is task-ux-404.
testCases: Cancel during the node-stop window re-emits status (renderer can resync)
Post-cutover cancel still hard-rejects unchanged
acceptance: A cancel request always produces a status emission the renderer can act on.
Post-cutover cancel rejection is preserved.

tasks.title: Native dialog is the single startup-interrupted recovery surface
tasks.description: When startup detects an unsafe interrupted cutover, make the native Electron blocking dialog the single authoritative recovery surface and remove the redundant React failed emission on that startup path, eliminating the two-competing-surfaces problem. Implements PRD D5a; closes gap #7. In-session failures are unaffected and keep using the React overlay.
implementationNotes: Remove the redundant React failed emission on the startup-interrupted path (`mithrilPartialSyncNodeStartup.ts:69-90`); keep the native dialog.showMessageBox with Wipe & full sync / Quit.
Honor the lock that startup-owned recovery must not depend on diagnostics UI or a running node and works before the renderer is ready.
Do not touch the in-session failure path, which must keep using the React overlay.
testCases: Startup-interrupted unsafe cutover shows only the native dialog (no React overlay)
In-session failure still shows the React overlay
acceptance: Only one surface (native dialog) handles startup-interrupted unsafe-cutover recovery.
In-session failure overlay behavior is unchanged.

phases.name: Renderer Discovery, Gating, And Confirmation
phases.description: Consume the availability read model, gate all partial-sync UI on it, add the proactive session-dismissible prompt with a deep-link into the confirmation modal, and replace the in-place confirmation row-swap with a proper decision-style modal. Implements PRD D1, D3 (renderer), D6, and the renderer half of D9's CTA re-arm.

tasks.title: Consume availability in the store, gate all partial-sync UI, and re-arm the CTA via isWorking
tasks.description: Wire MithrilPartialSyncStore to the availability read model (one-shot query at setup + periodic refresh while syncing) and gate ALL partial-sync UI on isEnabled: when the kill switch is off, the diagnostics recommendation/CTA and (later) the proactive prompt are fully hidden, never an enabled button that throws a raw rejection string. Additionally gate the recommendation/CTA on isSignificantlyBehind. Derive isActionBlocked from isWorking (in-flight phases) rather than isActive so terminal completed/cancelled/failed never block the CTA, re-arming it without an app restart. Implements PRD D1/D3 (renderer) and the CTA re-arm half of D9 (BUG1). Closes blocker #1's renderer half.
implementationNotes: Consume the availability channel from task-ux-101 in setup(); expose isEnabled, isSignificantlyBehind, behindByImmutables on the store.

Gate MithrilPartialSyncSection/Recommendation visibility on isEnabled (all UI) and additionally on isSignificantlyBehind (recommendation/CTA), so no dead control renders near the tip (D1 sign-off: hide, not disable).

Change isActionBlocked to derive from isWorking (in-flight phases) instead of `isActive = (status !== 'idle')` (`mithril-partial-sync.types.ts:92-94`), so completed/cancelled/failed re-arm the CTA immediately (D9/BUG1).
Keep disabling the CTA while any Mithril work is active (isActionBlocked still reflects the serialized mutation lock).
Honor the locked no-renderer-threshold rule: the renderer consumes booleans/figures, never computes the threshold.
Verify the existing _isTornDown guard keeps the status handler inert after teardown (D8/#15) and document that no IPC-abstraction unsubscribe is added.
testCases: Kill switch off hides all partial-sync UI (no enabled button)
isEnabled && isSignificantlyBehind shows the recommendation/CTA; otherwise hidden
Terminal completed/cancelled/failed re-arms the CTA (isActionBlocked clears)
CTA stays disabled while other Mithril work is active
acceptance: All partial-sync UI is hidden when the kill switch is off.
Recommendation/CTA appears only when isEnabled && isSignificantlyBehind.
isActionBlocked derives from isWorking so the CTA re-arms on terminal states without restart.
Renderer computes no threshold; the _isTornDown teardown guard is documented.

tasks.title: Proactive fast/standard fork prompt on the syncing screen with concise inline confirm
tasks.description: Add a fast/standard FORK prompt on the loading/sync screen (SyncingConnecting), shown only when isPartialSyncEnabled && isSignificantlyBehind && !proactivePromptDismissedThisSession. Primary 'Mithril Sync (fast)' (emphasized) opens a CONCISE INLINE CONFIRM step on the syncing screen ('Start now / Cancel') whose Start now invokes the existing partial-sync start path DIRECTLY -- no Diagnostics deep-link and no full review modal; confirmation still precedes start (lock #3) via the concise confirm. Secondary 'Standard Sync (slow)' sets an in-memory session flag (keeps normal syncing) and the copy notes the user can still start the Mithril sync later from Diagnostics. User-facing term is 'Mithril'/'Mithril Sync' (not 'partial sync'); display is epochs-only (sync-% dropped). Implements PRD D1 as amended by D13. Closes blocker #2's prompt half.
implementationNotes: PRD D13 redesign (explicit user sign-off 2026-06-25): the prompt is a fast/standard fork that confirms + starts IN PLACE, not a Diagnostics deep-link. DROP the deep-link plumbing entirely (no openDaedalusDiagnosticsDialog payload, no AppStore payload capture, no DaedalusDiagnosticsDialog showConfirmationOnOpen wiring); leave the already-shipped showConfirmationOnOpen prop on MithrilPartialSyncSection unused as a benign residual.

Mount a new presentational prompt component in SyncingConnecting (which is pure/props-only); gate on isPartialSyncEnabled && isSignificantlyBehind && !proactivePromptDismissedThisSession. NOTE the live store observable is `isPartialSyncEnabled` (MithrilPartialSyncStore.ts), fed by availability.isEnabled -- not a getter named isEnabled.

Prompt is two-state: choice view (epochs body + 'Mithril Sync (fast)' / 'Standard Sync (slow)') then a concise confirm view ('Mithril will stop your Cardano node, restore verified chain data, and restart it -- so you catch up faster.' + 'Start now' / 'Cancel'). Start now calls the SAME start path partial sync already uses (trace the start action used by DaedalusDiagnostics/MithrilPartialSyncSection; no new channel).

Add proactivePromptDismissedThisSession as an in-memory store flag + a dismiss action (same pattern as isCompletedOverlayDismissed); not persisted across restarts. 'Standard Sync (slow)' triggers the dismiss.

Behind-ness figure = RENDERER node-tip difference behindByEpochs = max(1, networkTip.epoch - localTip.epoch), computed in SyncingConnectingPage (mirror the task-ux-304 block in DaedalusDiagnostics.tsx). Keep a behind-unknown body fallback when tips/epoch are unavailable. NO sync-% (D13). NO 'immutable files' language.

Copy (approved EN; first-class JA, no placeholders): choice body L1 'Your node is about {epochs} epochs behind.' (unknown fallback 'Your node is behind the blockchain tip.'), L2 'Mithril can catch you up faster than the standard sync.'; handoff 'If skipped, you can still start the Mithril sync from the Diagnostics screen.'; buttons 'Mithril Sync (fast)' / 'Standard Sync (slow)'; confirm body 'Mithril will stop your Cardano node, restore verified chain data, and restart it -- so you catch up faster.'; confirm buttons 'Start now' / 'Cancel'. Preserve the locked 'verified ... chain data ... catch up faster' wording (#4).
Sprint-wide rollout of the 'Mithril' vocabulary and the sync-% removal across the confirmation modal / recommendation / overlay is task-ux-601, NOT this task.
testCases: Prompt appears only when enabled, significantly behind, and not yet dismissed this session
Choice view shows epochs body + fast/standard buttons; no sync-% and no 'immutable files' string
'Mithril Sync (fast)' reveals the concise confirm view; the fast button alone never starts the backend (confirmation precedes start)
'Start now' invokes the existing partial-sync start path directly (no Diagnostics navigation)
'Standard Sync (slow)' sets the in-memory session flag so the prompt does not reappear this session
behind-unknown fallback body renders when networkTip/localTip (or epoch) is unavailable
acceptance: A gated, session-dismissible fast/standard fork prompt renders on the syncing screen (epochs-only, 'Mithril' vocabulary).
'Mithril Sync (fast)' opens a concise inline confirm whose 'Start now' invokes the existing start path directly; no Diagnostics deep-link, no full review modal.
Confirmation precedes start (lock #3) is preserved via the concise confirm; no second un-confirmed start path.
'Standard Sync (slow)' dismiss is session-scoped (in-memory) and copy points to the manual Diagnostics path.

tasks.title: Replace in-place confirmation with a decision-style modal carrying behind-ness context
tasks.description: Replace the in-place prose row-swap with a proper focus-trapped modal: scrim, ESC/scrim-click = Back to diagnostics (not close the whole diagnostics dialog), a behind-ness context line from behindByImmutables, what-happens steps (stop node, download & verify, restart), the verified-data wording lock, and a primary Start / secondary Back-to-diagnostics button hierarchy. Stays lighter than bootstrap: no snapshot-selection and no storage-location picker; reuse bootstrap decision-view styling for visual consistency. Implements PRD D6; closes gaps #11, #14, #22, #23.
implementationNotes: Convert MithrilPartialSyncConfirmation from the in-place 560px row swap into a scrim + focus-trapped modal following existing Daedalus dialog patterns; do not invent a one-off modal system.
ESC / scrim-click = Back to diagnostics, not close the whole diagnostics dialog (fixes gap #23).

Behind-ness context line uses behindByImmutables (e.g., 'Your node is about N immutable files behind the latest verified snapshot'); renderer still does no thresholding. This closes gap #29 (no 'how far behind' copy) and the surfaced half of gap #26 (snapshot identity); full snapshot-metadata stays a non-goal.
Keep the locked 'verified Mithril data' wording (lock #4); keep copy simple (#34, no IOG-key/no-ancillary nuance).
Primary 'Start Mithril partial sync' vs secondary 'Back to diagnostics' with distinct styles (fixes gap #14, the shared-cancel-style bug).
Reuse bootstrap decision-view styling but NOT its snapshot-metadata grid; no snapshot-selection, no storage-location picker (locked non-goals).
Confirmation remains the only path to backend start (lock #3); it must support being opened on mount via showConfirmationOnOpen (deep-link from task-ux-302).

NOTE (D11/task-ux-304): the behind-ness copy shipped by this task ('about {count} immutable files behind') is REVISED by task-ux-304 to Cardano-native epochs (primary sentence) + a sync-% sentence; immutable files are an internal unit and are not shown to the user. This task remains the modal-structure source of truth; task-ux-304 owns the behind-ness figure and copy.
testCases: Confirmation renders as a focus-trapped modal with scrim
ESC / scrim-click returns to diagnostics without closing the whole dialog
Behind-ness line reflects behindByImmutables
Primary/secondary buttons have distinct styles
Confirming is the only path to backend start
acceptance: Confirmation is a focus-trapped decision-style modal with behind-ness context.
ESC / scrim-click = back to diagnostics, not full-dialog close.
Button hierarchy is primary/secondary with distinct styles.
No snapshot-selection or storage-location picker; verified-data wording preserved.
Confirming remains the only path to start.

tasks.title: User-facing behind-ness figure: epochs (node-tip epoch difference, renderer-computed) + sync-% context sentence
tasks.description: Replace the 'N immutable files behind' user-facing copy with Cardano-native language WITHOUT changing the behind-ness gate. Per PRD D12 (supersedes D11's figure source), the displayed figure is the EXACT node-tip epoch difference computed in the RENDERER -- behindByEpochs = Math.max(1, networkTip.epoch - localTip.epoch) read from NetworkStatusStore -- NOT a backend immutable->epochs conversion. No new backend field and no per-network filesPerEpoch constant are added. The confirmation modal (revising the copy shipped by the completed task-ux-303) renders 'Your node is about {epochs} epochs behind the blockchain tip.' as the primary sentence, then the current sync percentage as a SEPARATE trailing reference sentence (never interpolated mid-sentence), framed to convey the time/effort benefit of running partial sync versus waiting for normal sync. Immutable files remain an internal/engineering unit and are never shown to the user. The gate (isSignificantlyBehind / behindByImmutables) stays backend-owned and unchanged (D2 reaffirmed); node-tip distance >= the certified gap, so the figure can never show fewer epochs than the offer implies. Implements PRD D11 as amended by D12; reaffirms D2 and 'no renderer-computed threshold'. Establishes the single canonical behind-ness copy + node-tip figure reused by the proactive prompt (task-ux-302).
implementationNotes: PRD D12 supersedes D11's figure source: compute the figure in the RENDERER where the node tips already live. `behindByEpochs = Math.max(1, networkTip.epoch - localTip.epoch)`, guarding for null/missing tips or epoch. Use WHOLE-EPOCH subtraction (explicit user decision 2026-06-25), NOT a slot-fractional `round(deltaAbsoluteSlot / epochLength)`, for visual consistency with the 'Last network block'/'Last synchronized block' epoch rows shown beside it on Diagnostics. Floor at 1 whenever offered (node-tip distance >= the certified gap guarantees >= 1 when the gate fires).

Do NOT add a backend `behindByEpochs` field, a per-network `filesPerEpoch` table, or any change to `getPartialSyncBehindness` (`MithrilPartialSyncService.ts:677-706`) for display. The gate is unchanged and backend-owned: `isSignificantlyBehind` / `behindByImmutables` stay exactly as-is (D2 reaffirmed). `behindByImmutables` is INTERNAL only and is no longer consumed by the renderer for display (leaving it on the read model as a debug field or removing it is non-blocking cleanup, D12).

`DaedalusDiagnostics` already holds `localTip`/`networkTip` and `syncPercentage` and already passes `formattedSyncPercentage` + `behindByImmutables` into `MithrilPartialSyncSection` (`DaedalusDiagnostics.tsx:716-724`). Compute `behindByEpochs` there from the tips and pass it (alongside `formattedSyncPercentage`) down; stop using `behindByImmutables` for display.

Confirmation modal (`MithrilPartialSyncConfirmation.tsx`): add NET-NEW `behindByEpochs?: number` and `formattedSyncPercentage: string` props (replacing the `behindByImmutables` display prop). Change `mithrilPartialSyncConfirmationBehind` from '...{count} immutable files behind the latest verified snapshot.' to an epochs-first sentence, e.g. 'Your node is about {epochs} epochs behind the blockchain tip.', followed by a SEPARATE sentence using syncPercentage, e.g. 'Cardano node is currently {syncPercentage}% synced.' The percentage must be its own trailing reference sentence, never mid-sentence (explicit user directive 2026-06-24/25).

`MithrilPartialSyncSection` already RECEIVES `formattedSyncPercentage`; thread `behindByEpochs` + `formattedSyncPercentage` into the `<MithrilPartialSyncConfirmation>` element (`MithrilPartialSyncSection.tsx:118-124`; today only `behindByImmutables` is passed there).

Keep the `mithrilPartialSyncConfirmationBehindUnknown` fallback for when `networkTip`/`localTip` (or their `epoch`) are unavailable -- no fabricated number. Update message `description`s away from 'immutable-file gap' wording.

INTERACTION MODE = interactive_decision: the example EN+JA sentences here (epochs lead + separate sync-% sentence + benefit-vs-waiting framing) are illustrative. Pin the final wording with the user at implementation time (sprint copy-approval stop condition); benefit-framing is a judgment call the user cares about.

Frame the copy to convey the time/effort benefit of partial sync vs waiting for normal sync (explicit user directive 2026-06-24); keep the locked 'verified ... chain data ... catch up faster' wording (#4) and keep copy simple (#34).

Do NOT introduce a renderer-computed THRESHOLD. The gate stays backend-owned; this task only renders backend-provided booleans plus the epochs figure derived from the node tips the renderer already authoritatively displays.

No `.agent/system/api-endpoints.md` change is needed (no new IPC field); the figure is renderer-derived. Provide first-class EN+JA for the new/changed keys (no !!! placeholders); the holistic JA review is task-ux-601. NOTE: the existing ja-JP `mithrilPartialSyncConfirmationBehind` (ja-JP.json:160) literally says '約{count}個のimmutableファイル' (immutable files in JA) -- it MUST be replaced with the epochs+sync-% JA too, not just the EN. Use the i18n-messaging skill.
testCases: behindByEpochs = max(1, networkTip.epoch - localTip.epoch) is computed in the renderer from NetworkStatusStore tips (whole-epoch subtraction)
Figure is floored at 1 when offered and never shows fewer epochs than the offer implies (node-tip distance >= certified gap)
Confirmation modal renders epochs as the primary sentence and sync-% as a separate trailing sentence
Behind-unknown fallback renders when networkTip/localTip (or epoch) is unavailable (no fabricated number)
Gate is unchanged: isSignificantlyBehind still derives from the backend immutable gap >= THRESHOLD_IMMUTABLES
No backend behindByEpochs field or per-network filesPerEpoch constant is added
No 'immutable files' string is shown to the user in any behind-ness copy

tasks.description: Drive the download bar from filesDownloaded / filesTotal and label it as files (e.g., 'Restoring verified data - 142 / 980 files') in both flows, and stop both current misbehaviors: bootstrap's synthetic byte readout and partial sync feeding raw file counts into byte-named props. tasks.description: Make the overlay's recovery affordances match backend safety and wire the renderer half of success finalization. Disable Cancel during stopping-node with an explanatory tooltip ('Cancellation available once the node has stopped'); always call syncStatus() after any cancel so the UI never sticks (D5f renderer; pairs with task-ux-203 backend re-emit).

tasks.title: Mithril partial-sync UX remediation (manual-assessment fixes)
tasks.description: Net-new phase-7 remediation task gating the deployment QA gate (task-ux-702). The operator's manual assessment of the shipped Mithril partial-sync UX (task-ux-702-manual-assessment.md) surfaced 12 issues spanning a functional defect (the Mithril option invisible on first load), a locked-decision amendment (the completion flow), recovery/prompt UX, copy, theme contrast, and Storybook fidelity. 702a remediates all 12 grouped into 8 implementation categories (CAT-A...CAT-H), one code subagent per category, implemented sequentially on the shared working tree (CAT-A -> CAT-C -> CAT-B -> CAT-E -> CAT-F -> CAT-G -> CAT-D -> CAT-H), each followed by a code-review pass; all .scss/theme work is isolated in CAT-D. Two locked invariants are amended via sanctioned ADRs: D-702a-1 (auto-fire the D9 finalize on the completed-overlay timeout, replacing the 'Continue to Daedalus' click while keeping reset-to-idle + staging-folder removal + marker clear intact) and D-702a-2 (the backend stops emitting wipe-and-full-sync for the two pre-cutover cancel-originated arrays only; post-cutover wipe-only and the active-sync 3-action failure set are unchanged). Locked vocabulary holds (Mithril Sync / Standard Sync / Node Sync; epochs-only behind-ness with the behindUnknown fallback). EN + JA updated for every changed string (JA best-effort/provisional per the 601 glossary), with a final copy-overview deliverable for a manual Japanese translator (task-ux-702a-copy-overview.md, D-702a-7).
implementationNotes: CAT-A (ISSUE-1/2, renderer-only): first-load invisibility was a freshness bug -- MithrilPartialSyncStore re-fetched availability only `if (this.isWorking)`, false during normal sync. Drop the gate so `_refreshAvailability()` fires every 30s tick + a `_isRefreshingAvailability` re-entrancy guard; section self-corrects <=30s without reload. Add the reactive `NetworkStatusStore.isBehindnessKnown` computed (both tips finite-epoch; availability boolean, NOT a renderer threshold) consumed by CAT-F. The backend `getPartialSyncAvailability` probe-delay is flagged for the backend track (out of scope).

CAT-C (ISSUE-4, D-702a-4): simplify the confirmation body to two paragraphs (subject = epochs/behindUnknown line + restore sentence; subtext = the shared D-702a-6 canonical sentence) and remove Intro/StepStop/StepDownload/StepRestart/Success/Recovery copy. Create the single shared i18n key `daedalus.diagnostics.dialog.mithrilSyncProcessSummary`. Deliberately re-touches `mithrilPartialSyncConfirmationBehind` (previously 304/601-locked) -- recorded, not a regression.
CAT-B (ISSUE-3, D-702a-3): move the recommendation copy into a hover PopOver tooltip on the Mithril Sync button (verbatim D-702a-3 string), remove the buttonHintReady hint.

CAT-E (ISSUE-6, ADR D-702a-2): backend trims `wipe-and-full-sync` from the two pre-cutover cancel arrays only; button copy 'Retry Mithril Sync (fast)' / 'Restart Node Sync (slow)'; cancelled subtext -> body `

`; primary action right-aligned. Post-cutover wipe-only and the active-sync 3-action failure set unchanged (intentional asymmetry).

CAT-F (ISSUE-7, D-702a-5, depends on CAT-A): the prompt unmounted on the loading->Wallet Summary transition because it lived in SyncingConnecting. Hoist into a new app-level MithrilProactivePromptContainer (sibling of <Router>) so it persists; gate on CAT-A's `isBehindnessKnown` (anti-flash) + `status==='idle'`; 'Mithril Sync (fast)' primary; capitalize the subtitle; reuse the shared D-702a-6 key for the confirm view.

CAT-G (ISSUE-8, ADR D-702a-1): remove 'Continue to Daedalus'; the completed overlay shows a loading-style hand-off and the existing D9 finalize fires automatically on a completed-timeout via the unchanged `store.dismissCompletedOverlay`. Trigger swaps click->timeout; finalize cleanup (reset-to-idle + staging-folder removal + marker clear) byte-for-byte intact.

CAT-D (ISSUE-5/6-theme/9, owns all .scss + theme tokens): migrate the prompt card off report-issue tokens to --theme-mithril-*; fix confirmation white-on-white with standard --theme-dialog-* tokens (restyling the live `mithrilPartialSyncConfirmationRecovery` rule in place); brighten the cancelled hint; verify-first theme-token audit (zero token-value edits needed).

CAT-H (ISSUE-10/11/12, storybook-only): rename Nodes/Status -> Nodes/Diagnostic, move the dialogue views under Loading/Mithril, fix the download-progress-bar fixture (step-3 active anchor), add a partial-progress story, remove the redundant only-text Recommendation stories; the sidebar folder-vs-component icon is stock Storybook 6.4 behavior (report-only).

After all copy lands, the copy-overview subagent produces task-ux-702a-copy-overview.md for the manual Japanese translator (D-702a-7).

Do not relabel autonomous.

tasks.title: Mithril partial-sync UX cleanup (702a code-review remediation)
tasks.description: Net-new phase-7 cleanup task gating the deployment QA gate (task-ux-702). The xhigh code review of the uncommitted task-ux-702a working tree surfaced 14 actionable findings (#1-#14: 7 correctness/behavior, 1 test gap, 6 cleanups) plus 1 refuted non-bug (the recommendation tooltip on a disabled button, already covered by buttonHintBlocked - out of scope). A 2026-06-30 grill-with-docs CPU-cost re-validation added a 15th finding (#15) the 702a review under-traced, and a second 2026-06-30 grill-with-docs session (the 'epoch visibility' signalling defect) added a 16th finding (#16 / D-702b-10) that folds into the existing CAT-B/CAT-A/CAT-H (no new category). 702b remediates 14 + 1 (#15) + 1 (#16) = 16, grouped into 8 implementation categories (CAT-A...CAT-H), one code subagent each, implemented sequentially on the shared working tree in the collision-safe order CAT-B -> CAT-A -> CAT-C -> CAT-D -> CAT-E -> CAT-H -> CAT-F -> CAT-G, each followed by a code-review/compile pass. Decisions are locked in task-ux-702b-decisions.md (D-702b-0..10). Two decisions REVERSE/REFRAME the review: D-702b-1 KEEPS the proactive prompt's app-level mount and full cross-screen persistence (rejecting the review's route-scoping) and instead fixes the trigger (only once the Cardano node is loaded: networkStatus.isConnected, which implies past-verifying, plus networkStatus.isBehindnessKnown), adds a near-tip hide, and verifies the fixed banner does not obscure nav on Settings/Staking/Voting; D-702b-6 keeps the now-unconditional 30s availability poll (its isWorking guard removal was intentional for idle consumers) and instead bounds the request() with a timeout (anti-pin) and adds a known-stable back-off (poll FREQUENCY). D-702b-9 (CAT-H, grill-added #15) is the per-probe COST fix in the backend: the 30s availability probe (getPartialSyncBehindness) forks checkDiskSpace (df/PowerShell via getManagedChainPath -> getConfig -> getDefaultStorageConfig) AND re-reads the whole immutable/ dir (resolveLocalImmutableNumber) every tick, only the aggregator is cached; CAT-H caches the local immutable read (_getCachedLocalImmutableNumber) under the existing 5-min TTL so a cache hit skips BOTH, and invalidates both caches on lifecycle transitions. CAT-H is also the #16 producer. #16 (D-702b-10) re-sources the behind-by-epochs GATE + DISPLAY from cardano-wallet's late-resolving networkTip.epoch to the Mithril certified-beacon epoch (hybrid: prefer networkTip.epoch when finite, else certifiedEpoch): CAT-H extracts beacon.epoch (multi-path, undefined-safe) and returns certifiedEpoch on the behind-ness result + getPartialSyncAvailability IPC payload; CAT-B adds the certifiedEpoch? fallback param to computeBehindByEpochs; CAT-A populates the certifiedEpoch store observable in _applyAvailability and ORs certifiedKnown into the container gate. Undefined-safe => degrades to today's networkTip-only behavior when absent, so producer (CAT-H)/consumers (CAT-B/CAT-A) are order-independent; an operator verify (cardano-db snapshot show latest --json against the pinned-fork aggregator) confirms the beacon epoch key. No source code is edited by this task entry; the plan is task-ux-702b.md.
implementationNotes: CAT-B (#11/#7, D-702b-2): extract `computeBehindByEpochs(localTip, networkTip)` into utils/mithrilBehindness.ts (reusing isMithrilBehindnessKnown's finiteness check; returns undefined when networkEpoch - localEpoch <= 0 instead of Math.max(1,...) clamping). Switch BOTH call sites (MithrilProactivePromptContainer.tsx:45-52 + DaedalusDiagnostics.tsx:570-577) and delete the networkEpoch/localEpoch intermediates. DECOUPLING INVARIANT: the gate isMithrilBehindnessKnown stays clamp-free and UNCHANGED (true at equal epochs); the <=0 => undefined logic lives ONLY in the display-only computeBehindByEpochs. Consumers already have a behindUnknown fallback (MithrilPartialSyncConfirmation.tsx:67-99, SyncingConnectingMithrilPrompt.tsx:146-154), so the near-tip 'about 1 epochs behind' is fixed at the source.

CAT-A (#3/#4/#10/#14, D-702b-1/D-702b-3): KEEP the app-level mount + full cross-screen persistence (reverses the review). Add networkStatus.isConnected to the gate (node-loaded; isVerifyingBlockchain is !isConnected && ..., so isConnected implies past-verifying), keep networkStatus.isBehindnessKnown (anti-flash), and add a near-tip hide via `if (computeBehindByEpochs(...) === undefined) return null`. Add a SEPARATE session guard mithrilAttemptStartedThisSession (set in MithrilPartialSyncStore.startPartialSync, AND-!ed into the gate) so the prompt does not re-pop after an attempt ends, keeping proactivePromptDismissedThisSession single-purpose. Reorder render so the cheap boolean gate short-circuits before computing the figure (#14). Drop the dead `actions` inject (@inject('stores','actions') -> @inject('stores')) (#10).

CAT-C (#1/#5, D-702b-5): make MithrilErrorView render-order-agnostic (remove the orderedActions primary-last filter; render caller-supplied actions verbatim) and add an opt-in rightAlignActions prop + .actionsRightAligned scss modifier; change the shared .actions justify-content flex-end -> flex-start (opt-in, not global). Push ordering (secondary-first, primary-last) + the opt-in right-align into the MithrilPartialSyncOverlay caller only. RESTORE the bootstrap default untouched (MithrilBootstrap.tsx renders MithrilErrorView with no actions -> [Wipe(primary), Decline], left-aligned, primary-first). Fix MithrilDecisionView DOM order (secondaryAction before primaryAction, keeping flex-end => primary on the right). Correct the inaccurate 'sanctioned visual-only' note in task-ux-702a-impl-review.md:533-537.

CAT-D (#2, D-702b-4): renderer-only finalize robustness. In MithrilPartialSyncStore.dismissCompletedOverlay, await mithrilPartialSyncFinalizeChannel.request() BEFORE flipping isCompletedOverlayDismissed; on rejection keep the overlay up and resync via the try/finally { await this.syncStatus(); } pattern. Add a .catch to the overlay's completed auto-dismiss setTimeout callback (MithrilPartialSyncOverlay.tsx:84-91) so a finalize rejection is never unhandled. NO backend change (the service already runs _resetToIdleStatus before fs.remove, so failure ends at idle - the damage was the unhandled rejection + staging/.lock leak).

CAT-E (#6/#9, D-702b-6): keep the unconditional idle poll (do NOT restore the isWorking guard). Bound the availability request() with a timeout (Promise.race + cleared timer; AVAILABILITY_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS) so a wedged main process can never pin _isRefreshingAvailability. Add a known-stable back-off (slow the 30s poll to AVAILABILITY_REFRESH_BACKOFF_INTERVAL once disabled or enabled-and-not-behind; re-arm to 30s if it falls behind), preserving the first-load self-correct (ISSUE-1).

CAT-F (#12/#13, D-702b-7): extract a shared <CompletionBlock title detail? spinnerPosition> helper for the three near-identical blocks in MithrilProgressView.tsx (stopping :174 / starting :197 / completed-transition :220; completed has spinner-first and no detail). Call sites keep their own prop || intl.formatMessage(...) resolution; no i18n change. Delete the dead .primaryButton rule (SyncingConnectingMithrilPrompt.scss:62-68) and the two styles.primaryButton classNames refs (.tsx:174-179, :206-211); regen the .scss.d.ts (rendered width unchanged via .actionButton).

CAT-G (#8, D-702b-8): add a live spec on the REAL MithrilPartialSyncOverlay driven from status:'failed' with canRetry:false/canRestartNormally:false/canWipeAndFullSync:true (wipe resolves to primary): assert the wipe button renders, click it, assert onWipeAndFullSync fired once. Drive from failed, NOT cancelled (D-702a-2 removed wipe from the pre-cutover cancelled dialogue).

CAT-H (#15, D-702b-9, grill-added 2026-06-30, BACKEND-ONLY): the 30s availability probe getPartialSyncBehindness (MithrilPartialSyncService.ts:669-698) forks checkDiskSpace (df/PowerShell) via getManagedChainPath -> getConfig -> getDefaultStorageConfig (chainStorageManagerConfig.ts:23 - NOT the start() preflight) AND re-reads the whole immutable/ dir via resolveLocalImmutableNumber every tick; only the aggregator (_getCachedLatestCertifiedImmutableNumber) is cached. Add _localImmutableCache + a private _getCachedLocalImmutableNumber() mirroring the aggregator cache (reuse the existing 5-min PARTIAL_SYNC_BEHINDNESS_CACHE_TTL_MS) so a cache HIT skips BOTH getManagedChainPath (no fork) and resolveLocalImmutableNumber (no readdir); the behind-ness result stays computed fresh (cachedLatest - cachedLocal). Add _invalidateBehindnessCaches() (nulls both caches) called at start()/cancel() entry and inside _resetToIdleStatus() (covers restart-normal/wipe/finalize-wipe/finalize-completed) so post-cutover behind-ness is fresh. Complements CAT-E (frequency); no ISSUE-1 regression (stale-low local reads as MORE behind = conservative). Mirror the existing aggregator-cache spec at MithrilPartialSyncService.spec.ts:1271-1292.

#16 (D-702b-10, grill-added 2026-06-30, spans CAT-B/CAT-A/CAT-H): re-source the behind-by-epochs GATE + DISPLAY from cardano-wallet's late-resolving networkTip.epoch to the Mithril certified-beacon epoch. CAT-H: add extractCertifiedEpoch (multi-path, undefined-safe - mirror extractLatestCertifiedImmutableNumber's explicitPaths via getNestedValue + toPositiveInteger; the REAL existing immutable fixture is cardano_db_beacon: { immutable_file_number: '25' } at mithrilSnapshotMetadata.spec.ts:30-32, so add an epoch to that same shape), set certifiedEpoch on ResolvedLatestSnapshot in normalizeResolvedLatestSnapshot, carry epoch on _latestCertifiedImmutableCache, expose a sibling private _getCachedCertifiedEpoch(): number | null, and APPEND certifiedEpoch to the success (:686-689) and gap<=0 (:682-685) returns via a conditional spread ...(certifiedEpoch != null ? { certifiedEpoch } : {}) - keep behindByImmutables: gap as the existing unconditional literal (do NOT refactor it into a spread). The != null guard is required because _getCachedCertifiedEpoch returns null (not undefined) and Jest toEqual ignores undefined but not null; only TWO of the four spec cases carry behindByImmutables (:1228 true/20, :1241 false/5), :1254/:1266 are { isSignificantlyBehind: false }. MithrilController.getPartialSyncAvailability auto-forwards certifiedEpoch via { isEnabled, ...behindness } (VERIFY-ONLY, no edit). CAT-B: add the certifiedEpoch? fallback param to computeBehindByEpochs (prefer networkTip.epoch when finite, else certifiedEpoch; the named isMithrilBehindnessKnown stays byte-identical) + declare the certifiedEpoch store observable. CAT-A: add certifiedEpoch? to the MithrilPartialSyncAvailability IPC payload type, set this.certifiedEpoch in _applyAvailability, and OR certifiedKnown into the container gate. Undefined-safe => degrades to today's networkTip-only behavior when absent, so producer/consumers are order-independent. OPERATOR VERIFY-ONLY: run cardano-db snapshot show latest --json against the pinned-fork aggregator (flake.nix:23) to confirm the beacon epoch key BEFORE writing extractCertifiedEpoch's explicitPaths; #16 is not done until the live key is confirmed (else the early-sync fix is silently inert).

Refuted non-bug (out of scope): the recommendation tooltip on a disabled button is covered by the always-visible buttonHintBlocked; the optional one-line <span>-wrapper polish for consistency with MithrilProgressView.tsx:248-254 is NOT required.

interactive_validation: decisions are locked in the grill record; the human gates are the per-category code-review pass and operator visual validation of the two verify-only items (the D-702b-1 fixed-banner-over-Settings/Staking/Voting caveat and the D-702b-5 empty-chain bootstrap dialog). Node v24 renderer verify-env caveat: regen .scss.d.ts via typed-scss-modules + the gitignored identity-obj-proxy jest sidecar before treating tsc/jest fails as regressions.
testCases: computeBehindByEpochs returns undefined at diff <= 0 and the diff when > 0; isMithrilBehindnessKnown is unchanged (true at equal epochs) (CAT-B)
The proactive prompt is gated on networkStatus.isConnected + isBehindnessKnown + computeBehindByEpochs > 0, persists across routes, and never re-pops after an attempt starts this session (CAT-A)

MithrilErrorView renders caller-supplied action order verbatim; the empty-chain bootstrap default is restored (primary-first, left-aligned) while the partial-sync overlay opts into secondary-first/primary-right; MithrilDecisionView DOM order is secondary-before-primary (CAT-C)
dismissCompletedOverlay awaits finalize before flipping isCompletedOverlayDismissed and resyncs on rejection with no unhandled promise rejection; the overlay timeout callback is caught (CAT-D)
A never-settling availability request() no longer pins _isRefreshingAvailability (timeout clears it); the idle poll still fires and backs off once known-stable (CAT-E)
The three MithrilProgressView completion blocks render identically via the shared CompletionBlock; the dead .primaryButton CSS + refs are gone with unchanged button width (CAT-F)
A live overlay test driven from failed renders the wipe button and fires onWipeAndFullSync once (CAT-G)

Within the 5-min TTL, repeated getPartialSyncBehindness() calls resolve getManagedChainPath/resolveLocalImmutableNumber (hence checkDiskSpace + readdir) once, not per call; after the TTL or a lifecycle transition the next probe re-resolves them; the behind-ness verdict per (latest, local) pair is unchanged (CAT-H)

#16: a beacon carrying { epoch, immutable_file_number } makes getPartialSyncBehindness return certifiedEpoch and getPartialSyncAvailability forward it; a beacon without an epoch yields certifiedEpoch null/absent with the immutable verdict unchanged (the four existing toEqual cases stay green via the != null spread). computeBehindByEpochs returns the certified diff when networkTip is null but certifiedEpoch is finite and > localTip.epoch, and prefers networkTip.epoch when finite; the proactive prompt renders early-sync on the certified anchor (CAT-H/CAT-B/CAT-A)
acceptance: All 14 actionable 702a-review findings (#1-#14) plus the grill-added #15 and #16 are remediated across CAT-A...CAT-H with a per-category code-review APPROVED; the refuted tooltip is documented as out-of-scope.

The proactive prompt keeps its app-level mount + cross-screen persistence (review's route-scoping reversed), triggers only once the node is loaded (isConnected + isBehindnessKnown), hides near the tip, and never re-pops after an attempt (separate mithrilAttemptStartedThisSession guard).
Gate-vs-display decoupling holds: isMithrilBehindnessKnown stays clamp-free/unchanged; the <=0 => undefined logic lives only in computeBehindByEpochs (display).
The shared MithrilErrorView is order-agnostic, the empty-chain bootstrap default is restored, and the inaccurate 'visual-only' note in task-ux-702a-impl-review.md is corrected.

The finalize fix is renderer-only (await-before-hide + catch + resync; no backend reorder); the availability poll keeps its idle behavior, bounds request() against pinning, and backs off when known-stable.

CAT-H (#15): the per-probe backend cost is deduped - the availability probe no longer forks checkDiskSpace or re-reads the immutable/ dir on every tick (local read cached under the existing 5-min TTL, both caches invalidated on lifecycle transitions); the offer signal value is unchanged and the catch-up window (where CAT-E's back-off is gated off) is now cheap per tick.

#16 (D-702b-10): the behind-by-epochs gate + display are re-anchored on the Mithril certified-beacon epoch (hybrid: networkTip.epoch when finite, else certifiedEpoch), so during early/mid sync (networkTip null) the prompt + Diagnostics surface the figure instead of staying suppressed; isMithrilBehindnessKnown stays byte-identical, certifiedEpoch is plumbed probe -> IPC payload -> store -> container, and an absent beacon epoch degrades to today's networkTip-only behavior (operator verify confirms the live key).

tasks.title: Full manual QA matrix and rollout-readiness decision (deployment gate)
tasks.description: After automated coverage is green and backend-correctness re-validation passes, run the full manual QA matrix: all 3 networks (mainnet, preprod, preview) x default + custom chain storage x success/cancel/retry/restart-normal/wipe-and-full-sync recovery paths x packaged builds on Windows/macOS/Linux. Re-test the six task-401 regressions individually (gap #18). Record results in the plan workspace, finalize the production default and any network-scoping of the kill switch, and finalize the rollback checklist. This branch does not merge to master / ship until this matrix passes. Implements the QA gate of PRD D7 and the Rollout section.
implementationNotes: manual_execution: requires human-driven validation on supported networks, custom storage, packaged builds, and recovery paths; do not relabel autonomous.
Record environment, platform, network, chain-storage target, and observed behavior for every QA pass.
Re-test the six task-401 regressions individually rather than via one combined pass (gap #18). Completing this matrix is the disposition for gap #3 (the previously-deferred manual QA matrix).
Decide the production default and network-scoping of mithrilPartialSyncEnabled (deferred from D3) and whether the feature ships enabled, guarded, or held back.
Finalize the rollback checklist: the kill switch hides all partial-sync UI when off while the startup-owned native recovery dialog remains for already-interrupted unsafe-cutover installs.
testCases: All 3 networks x default + custom storage x recovery paths validated
Packaged builds validated on Windows/macOS/Linux
Six task-401 regressions individually re-tested
acceptance: Manual QA results are captured in repo-local planning artifacts.
The production default / network-scoping decision for the kill switch is documented.
A rollback checklist is written for release review.
A clear merge/ship readiness decision is recorded.

riskAreas: Preserving every locked safety boundary while changing renderer gating and overlay behavior (allowedRecoveryActions-only rendering, confirmation precedes start, no renderer threshold).

Amending locked decision #16 (D9): the success-finalization sequence must keep the success screen visible until dismiss while resetting to idle, cleaning staging, and stamping the node-start-verified marker exactly once.
Shared MithrilProgressView/MithrilStepIndicator changes (D4) must not regress the bootstrap success path.
Staging relocation (D7/BUG3) must remain staged-only and fail closed on insufficient disk space.
Referenced backend-correctness gates (gaps #16/#17/#18/#19) and the full QA matrix are deployment prerequisites that require operator validation and are not fully specified by this PRD.
summary.testingNotes: Each feature task carries its own focused Jest/Storybook coverage; task-ux-503 closes cross-cutting gaps (diagnostics->overlay handoff, bootstrap file-count progress, gating, success finalization, live activity affordances, recovery actions). Automated verification must be green before task-ux-702 manual QA. The full manual QA matrix (3 networks x default+custom storage x recovery paths x packaged builds) is the deployment gate and is human-executed.
summary.rollbackPlan: mithrilPartialSyncEnabled remains the rollout lever; exposed to the renderer so all partial-sync UI hides when off. This branch enables it for manual testing; the production default and network-scoping are deferred to the readiness gate (task-ux-702). The startup-owned native recovery dialog remains available for any already-interrupted unsafe-cutover install even when the diagnostics entry is disabled.
securityNotes: Live chain data integrity remains the primary safety constraint; never render recovery actions the backend did not authorize via allowedRecoveryActions.
Never route raw mithril-client JSON strings into UI copy; map structured error codes to localized copy instead.
Keep support-log paths and error surfacing detailed enough for incident review without leaking unrelated secrets.
